topic.what: Cross-session threat detection benchmark for AI agents where session-bound guardrails fail against multi-session attacks
topic.how: Information-bottleneck framing between message stream and downstream correlator LLM; bounded-memory Coreset Memory Reader ranks and retains highest-signal cross-session fragments
topic.why_matters: AI-agent guardrails are memoryless; attacks that spread payload across dozens of sessions slip past session-bound detectors; GTG-1002 cyber-espionage campaign demonstrates real-world impact

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to detect cross-session attacks before action-on-objective fires
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs Full-Log Correlator (50.0%, 13/26) and per-session judge (53.8%, 14/26)
characteristics.evidence: Table 15: Coreset reader holds 19/26 recall on cross_session vs drops to 13-14/26 for LLM-only readers
characteristics.context: 26 taxonomies across dilution and cross_session shards; statistical power limited at n=26 per class

characteristics.what_it_measures: False alarms on benign-pristine scenarios (no confounders)
False alarms on benign-hard scenarios (confounder patterns)
characteristics.context: 14 benign-hard scenarios with approval_fatigue and tacit_collusion confounders

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cross-session detection at action point recall
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 0% on T05, T10, T26 (aggregate-only scenarios)
characteristics.evidence: Table 17: CSDA@100% on 26 attack scenarios; Full-Log Correlator detects 23/26 on dilution
characteristics.context: 26 taxonomies; Full-Log Correlator loses to dilution on 3 aggregate-only scenarios

characteristics.value: 0.690 on cross_session shard for Coreset reader
characteristics.evidence: Table 15: Coreset CSTM=0.690 cross_session vs 0.775 dilution
characteristics.context: Non-compensatory composite; CSR_prefix term penalizes ranker reshuffling that breaks KV-cache reuse

characteristics.what_it_measures: Full-Log Correlator token usage vs 1M context window
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 0% truncation rate (no scenarios exceed window)
characteristics.evidence: Table 18: p95=11.0%, max=14.8% context utilization; 0/54 scenarios truncated
characteristics.context: Confirms dilution degradation is information-theoretic, not context-window artifact

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cumulative input tokens for Full-Log Correlator across 54 scenarios
characteristics.evidence: Table 18: SIEM input tokens total=2,773,348; mean/p50/p95/max per scenario reported
characteristics.context: Scales linearly with scenario count; per-scenario headroom survives but cumulative would hit window 1/3 through 54-scenario pass

good_for: comparing bounded vs unbounded memory approaches for AI agents
detecting slow-drip and compositional attacks across sessions
not_for: precision rankings of specific models or prompts
detecting T08, T14, T22, T25, T26 on cross_session shard (5/26 undetected by all LLM-only readers)
requires: bounded coreset buffer (K=50 default, plug-in ranker)
identity anchors for policy-grounded ground truth

novelty: First benchmark for cross-session threat detection (CSTM-Bench) with 26 taxonomies across 7 identity anchors
Information-bottleneck framing isolating architectural choice from algorithm choice
inject_on_reader operation: benign per-session turns compile into correlator-targeted prompt injection
CSTM composite metric fusing detection F1 with CSR_prefix (serving-cost proxy, LLM-free/label-free)
Cross_session shard via closed-loop adversarial rewriter that softens surface phrasing while preserving cross-session artifacts verbatim
